body.title: [sprint] DS.0 can't close ANY real ticket — _post_result does a bare close that proof-on-close refuses
body.intention: I intend that DS.0's listener closes a completed ticket through the proof-on-close gate — emitting a proof or closing --shipped-unproven with a named lever — so DS.0 can actually COMPLETE a real build instead of escalating at the close step every time.
body.description:
Verified 2026-07-03 (reading device.py + repeated live proof-on-close refusals). unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/device.py:285 _post_result closes via _run_queue_cmd('close', ticket_id, note) — a BARE close (no proof, no --shipped-unproven). The proof-on-close gate REFUSES a bare close (returns non-zero), so _run_queue_cmd returns None -> line 286 -> ticket still sprint -> _escalate_ticket('close command failed'). Net: DS.0 escalates the close on EVERY real ticket -> can't complete any. (Today's smoke build bypassed _post_result, so this wasn't hit.) THE fix site is the listener, NOT the builder prompt (advisor caught this — the prompt's steps 5-6 are vestigial; the listener owns the close). DS.0 needs proof-on-close awareness: for a builder run, default to close --shipped-unproven naming the missing proof-lever (a small model can't reliably author a HEAD-valid proof yet), OR wire proof_emitter when a test node is identifiable. Reconcile with the builder prompt (T-ds-builder-prompt-fixes) so the model isn't ALSO trying to close (double).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/device.py (_post_result), possibly worker_listener.py.
**Design rules:** proof-on-close (every close proven-or-shipped-unproven); log-every-state-change (close is a state change).
**Scope boundary:** IN — make _post_result close through the proof-on-close gate (default shipped-unproven with a lever, or proof when wired). OUT — the review-ladder (T-ds-advisor-review-ladder); prompt fixes (T-ds-builder-prompt-fixes).
**Test plan:** unit — _post_result on a DONE: result closes the ticket (via shipped-unproven path) instead of escalating 'close command failed' (red: bare close refused -> escalate; green: closed with a named lever). Integration: DS.0 full-cycle on a real ticket reaches closed, not escalated.
**Completion criteria:** DS.0 closes a completed real ticket (shipped-unproven with a lever, or proven) rather than escalating at close; a test guards it.
**Rollback:** git revert restores the bare close.

description: **Akien decision (2026-07-03): FULL proof-on-close path.** DS routes its close through the SAME proof-vs-shipped-unproven decision CC uses (choose per ticket) — NOT a blanket shipped-unproven default. The listener attempts a real proof where the ticket admits one (DS builds test cases, so red->green is natural for that domain) and falls back to `close --shipped-unproven <lever>` only when no HEAD-valid proof can be authored. Most-correct long-term shape; scope now INCLUDES wiring proof-emission into the _post_result listener path, not just swapping the bare close for shipped-unproven.
